dataset: OSCAR EMR (Open Source Clinical Application and Resource)

pi_items.source: Verified via web search: Dermacool 1% Menthol in Aqueous Cream, Menthoderm 1% Menthol in Aqueous Cream, and David Hosking Menthol 1% Cream are real products. https://www.chemist.net/dermacool-1-menthol-in-aqueous-cream-500g-pd-12483

Verified via Drugs.com, Wikipedia, PubChem, and pharmaceutical references. Menthol is the correct generic/active pharmaceutical ingredient name (chemical formula C10H20O). https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/compound/Menthol

Verified via web search for available menthol cream formulations. Multiple manufacturers produce menthol 1% cream formulations including Dermacool and Menthoderm products. https://www.chemist.net/dermacool-1-menthol-in-aqueous-cream-500g-pd-12483
Verified via Drugs.com dosage guides and pharmaceutical references. Cream is a standard dosage form for topical menthol products. https://www.drugs.com/dosage/menthol-topical.html

Verified via multiple pharmaceutical databases (RxList, Drugs.com, Mayo Clinic, Cleveland Clinic). Menthol 1% cream is consistently documented as a topical application. https://www.rxlist.com/menthol_topical/generic-drug.htm

privileged_info_summary: The task involves adding a custom medication entry to a containerized OSCAR EMR test database. The medication details are based on real pharmaceutical information: Menthol 1% Cream is a genuine pharmaceutical product available from multiple manufacturers (Dermacool, Menthoderm, David Hosking), with menthol as the verified active ingredient, 1% as a standard concentration, cream as the dosage form, and topical as the route of administration. The test environment runs OSCAR EMR (Canadian open-source EMR system) in Docker containers, making the database environment synthetic, though the medication specifications are realistic and match actual marketed products.
